qas.q: Upon your release, a DoD Public Affairs Officer (PAO) will be available to help you. Their goal is to ________________.
qas.a:
•Help you regain control over your life (correct)
•Coordinate your interactions with the media (correct)

qas.q: The evasion plan of action (EPA) provides recovery forces the following information should you become isolated.
qas.a:
•Your evasion intentions and key recovery information (correct)
•It helps them to anticipate your actions and intentions (Correct)
•Details the methods and procedures you intend to use to communicate with recovery forces (correct)

qas.q: To which article of the code of conduct does the following statement refer? If captured I will continue to resist by all means available. I will make every effort to escape and aid others to escape. I will accept neither parole nor special favors from the enemy.
Which evasion aids can assist you with making contact with the local population?
The code of conduct describes your basic responsibilities and obligations from isolation to captivity resolution.

Limiting the amount of personal information available to others includes reducing your __________ footprint, and personal information items not required for your operational mission such as credit cards and other identifiable items in your wallet or purse.
The code of conduct _________ for military members when isolated or held against their will by entities hostile to the U.S.
One way to gain acceptance from the local population after making contact is to discuss the similarities between your religions. This helps to show you are peaceful and mean no harm.
Identify some posture and resistance constants of an effective resistance posture.
Which of the following are criteria for selecting a shelter site?
qas.a:
•The area is large enough for you and your equipment
•Provides natural protection from environment, hazards, and threats
•Sufficient materials are available for constructing a shelter

qas.q: Identify elements of article II of the code of conduct.
qas.a:
•During military operations outside declared hostilities you may delay contact with local authorities
•Military members are never authorized to surrender

qas.q: The three basic food sources during isolation are packed rations, animals and insects, and plants.
When are personnel ALWAYS authorized to escape?
The two types of reporting isolating events are observer-reported and media reporting.
You should attempt to provide proof of life during any audio or video recording or written documents.

The weakened state of the captives' __________ reduces their capacity to collectively resist adversary exploitation efforts. Acceptance of special favors also provides an opening for an adversary's future exploitation efforts against you.
As part of your resistance posture you should portray ______ at all times.
Actions to take when capture is imminent include.
qas.a:
•Sanitize personal or sensitive materials
• Try to retain your personal cell phone
•Leave evidence of presence at capture point
•communicate your situation
(All are correct)

qas.q: What evasion aid is tailored to cover an individual operational area, combining standard navigation charts and maps with evasion and survival information?
Should your captors provide an opportunity to communicate using written, oral, or video means, you should:

Selecting a signaling site and putting your signal in a suitable location is critical. A signaling site is defined as any site, based on your situation that enhances signaling efforts and the likelihood for the signal to be detected by friendly forces. What is a signaling site criterion?
qas.a:
~Recovery possible from/near site

~Sufficient materials for signal construction

~Site hidden from potential enemy observation and ground fire

qas.q: During a direct action recovery, you should pick up a weapon and assist the recovery team.
The senior ranking military member should never take command of all prisoners, in all captivity situations, to ensure survivability.
During a hole-up what is your primary concern?
When providing proof of life what information should you include
qas.a:
•A verifiable date
•You and your fellow captives' identities
•Your health and welfare

qas.q: Continuously learning about your captivity environment and the captor is known as ___________________.
What should you do during USG negotiations for your release?
qas.a:
•Maintain your honor and communicate your innocence
•Remain professional and avoid exploitation
•Have faith in the USG

qas.q: Tactical hand-held radio transceivers, cell phones, satellite phones, and personal locator beacons (PLBs) are devices you could use for electronic communication.
What are some de-escalation strategies personnel can use to discourage captor violence?
qas.a:
•Maintain a low profile
•Be conscious of your body language

qas.q: Which of the following are used to control bleeding?
qas.a:
•Use a tourniquet
•Elevate and immobilize
•Apply direct pressure

qas.q: Leaving evidence of your presence supports U.S. Government effort to locate, identify and recover you.

To which article of the code of conduct does the following statement refer? When questioned, should I become a prisoner of war, I am required to give name, rank, service number and date of birth. I will evade answering further questions to the utmost of my availability. I will make no oral or written statements disloyal to my country and its allies or harmful to their cause.
Identify steps in building a fire.
qas.a:
•Carefully place small, dry kindling over the burning tinder
•Prepare adequate amounts of tinder, kindling, and fuel
•Use a platform by

qas.q: In the event of isolation during operations other than war, the reasons to delay contact with legitimate authorities include
What are the goals of using a disguise
What pre-deployment document requires you to provide, four personal authentication statements, full front and right side photographs, and fingerprints?
What are some methods to purify water
qas.a:
1.Chemically treating water with chlorine or iodine 2.Purifying with a commercial micro filter
3.Boiling vigorously for at least one minute

qas.q: You should limit your communication with the captor to which of the following?
qas.a:
1.Address health and welfare concerns
2.Ask to be returned to U.S. control
3.Provide the innocent circumstances leading to capture
4.Identify yourself

qas.q: Resistance to exploitation is a battle of wits with your captor.

An effective memory tool that can assist you with using situational awareness during an isolating event is the acronym PAID-E, which stands for perceive, ______________, interpret, decide, and execute.
